name: Ride to the Prosperina Reservoir
region_intro: Jogging around Mérida offers a unique experience across a predominantly flat landscape, making it accessible for various fitness levels. The region is characterized by its urban parks, tree-lined boulevards, and natural trails that often lead to reservoirs or along river systems. While the city itself is largely flat, the surrounding areas provide diverse environments for running, including natural reserves and coastal paths. Best jogging routes around Mérida The most popular jogging route is Ride to the Prosperina Reservoir, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes about 36 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the reservoir and surrounding natural areas. Another top favourite among local runners is Guadiana Natural Trail, a Difficult 10.3 miles (16.5 km) path. This trail follows the natural course of the Guadiana river, providing a scenic and challenging running experience. Local runners also love the Cornalvo Reservoir Trail, a 4.7 miles (7.5 km) trail leading through natural parkland around the reservoir, often completed in about 51 minutes. Jogging around Mérida is defined by flat urban paths, natural trails, and reservoir-side routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy park loops to more challenging long-distance runs. The routes in Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Mérida's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Mérida?
A: There are over 70 dedicated running routes around Mérida, offering a wide range of options for all fitness levels. Nearly 1000 runners have used komoot to explore the diverse terrain.

Q: Are there easy jogging routes for beginners in Mérida?
A: Yes, Mérida offers several easy jogging routes perfect for beginners. While the majority are moderate, there are 4 routes specifically rated as easy. The city's predominantly flat terrain makes it very accessible for new runners.

Q: What are some popular spots for running within Mérida city?
A: Within Mérida, popular spots include the iconic &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Montejo&lt;/strong&gt;, a tree-lined boulevard perfect for scenic runs, and &lt;strong&gt;La Plancha Park&lt;/strong&gt;, a recently renovated central park with dedicated paths. For a more structured run, the &lt;strong&gt;Salvador Alvarado Stadium&lt;/strong&gt; offers a world-class 1 km track.

Q: Can I find circular running routes near Mérida?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes available. For example, you could try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282211211/run-to-zona-de-nidos-de-cigueenas-rio-guadiana-circular-desde-merida" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Run to Zona de Nidos de Cigüeñas, Río Guadiana — circular desde Mérida&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.6 km loop, or the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280289157/el-rugidero-y-la-presa-de-cornalvo-circular-desde-la-presa-de-cornalvo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Rugidero y la Presa de Cornalvo — circular desde la Presa de Cornalvo&lt;/a&gt;, which is 18.3 km.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect on Mérida's running trails?
A: Mérida's running trails offer a mix of urban paths, natural trails, and reservoir-side routes. You'll find lush vegetation, tropical forests, and wetlands in areas like the Cuxtal Ecological Reserve, or scenic views around reservoirs such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3071953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by historical sites or landmarks?
A: Yes, Mérida is rich in history. Some routes may offer glimpses of ancient structures. Notable historical landmarks in the area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Temple of Diana&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3813989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Milagros Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3775155" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge of Mérida&lt;/a&gt;. Exploring the ancient Mayan ruins like Uxmal also offers an outdoor cultural experience.

Q: What is the best time of day or year to go jogging in Mérida?
A: Mérida is hot and humid year-round, so it's best to go jogging during the cooler parts of the day. Early mornings or near sunset are ideal to avoid the intense midday heat and enjoy a more comfortable run.

Q: What do other runners say about the jogging routes in Mérida?
A: The running routes in Mérida are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 7 reviews. Runners often praise the predominantly flat terrain, the scenic reservoir views, and the variety of urban and natural paths available.

Q: Are there any long-distance or challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For experienced runners seeking a challenge, Mérida offers 20 difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280676889/guadiana-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 16.5 km path that follows the natural course of the Guadiana river, providing a scenic and demanding experience.

Q: Can I combine my run with other outdoor activities in the Mérida region?
A: Yes, the Mérida region is fantastic for combining running with other outdoor activities. You can explore nearby cenotes for swimming, visit the Ría Celestún Nature Reserve for birdwatching, or enjoy hiking trails in the Cuxtal Ecological Reserve. The coastal town of Progreso, just 30 minutes away, also offers beaches for watersports.

Q: Are there any running trails that offer views of water bodies?
A: Many routes in Mérida feature beautiful water views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992989932/ride-to-the-prosperina-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to the Prosperina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992978510/cornalvo-reservoir-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cornalvo Reservoir Trail&lt;/a&gt; both offer scenic paths around these impressive water bodies. You can also find viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of the Alange Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: About five kilometers northwest of the city is this Roman reservoir, in the same place where the famous battle of Albuera took place. In it, the dispute over the throne of Castile that she had with Juana “la Beltraneja” was settled in favor of Isabel the Catholic.
